item.title: Posting this again for awareness
item.description: https://antifreeze.app/ "But here's the thing. When you Google "ICE tracking app" or "ICE sighting alerts," AntiFreeze doesn't show up. The entire first page is articles about ICEBlock, which doesn't even exist anymore. The app Apple killed is still dominating search results, and the one that was specifically built to survive exactly what happened to ICEBlock is invisible. I'm not asking for money. The app is free. I don't run ads. I don't collect data. I built this because the tools communities were relying on had a single point of failure, and that failure happened exactly the way everyone should have expected it to. What I am asking is that people know this exists. Share the link. Tell your local immigrant rights organizations. Tell your rapid response networks. Tell anyone who used to rely on ICEBlock and thinks there's nothing left." https://www.dailykos.com/stories/2026/3/20/2374166/-I-built-an-ICE-tracking-app-that-can-t-be-pulled-from-the-App-Store-Because-it-was-never-in-one
item.dc:creator: azazyel

item.title: Jane Doe cosplay (ZZZ)
item.description: If I had a dollar for everytime someone asked me to cosplay her, I'd have enough to buy at least one copy of GTA6 (it's funny bc it's true) And since she's the character I started playing Zenless Zone Zero for, I decided to finally give it a try :D PS. You can also find me on: • Reddit: https://reddit.com/user/aniku_ • Insta: https://instagram.com/anna_himejima (casual account)

item.title: Maine residents are marching on the streets after ICE murdered a 26-year-old Colombian man who was authorized to work in the US and had a social security number.
item.dc:creator: FunChatAnime

item.title: Murderer.
item.description:
The difference of a socialist and a moderate corporation Democrat.
Fox News & Co try to dehumanize the people. His name was Joan Sebastian Guerrero. - He was here legally. - He was authorized to work. - He had Social Security Number. - He was NOT target of ICE investigation. - He did NOT try to kill ICE agents.
They are going to keep murdering people if they don’t believe there will be repercussions.
You can help stopping them! Take action by visiting https://icerr.com/. Select your state to find a local organization near you. These groups have 'ICE Response Teams' that text or call you when agents are in the city, allowing you to document and film their activities. They are also looking for volunteers to conduct active patrols to locate ICE agents, or to call a hotline to warn others if you happen to stumble across them. At least get the hotline number.
item.dc:creator: onushka

item.title: Trump Responsible for Cyclospora
item.description: This is 100% true In July 2025, the CDC scaled back its premier active tracking system for foodborne illnesses, FoodNet (the Foodborne Diseases Active Surveillance Network). The agency reduced the list of pathogens requiring strict, active tracking from eight down to just two.

item.title: Liz Oyer (@lawyer_oyer): We need to talk about antitrust law. It’s a crucial protection for consumers against corporate profiteering. DOJ is responsible for enforcement, but Todd Blanche’s Justice Department is refusing to do so.

item.title: 21 nice and wholesome things to brighten your day, vol 311
item.description:
A Bee Fell Asleep in my Sunflower ~u/DrDrillbit
There's been a few bees I recognize that love my one bloomed sunflower. They're having a field day and one even slept in the flower overnight - pictured. I can't wait to see how they react when the others bloom. ~u/DrDrillbit
~u/saky12
~u/Cmmonbro

item.title: GovBrief Today #529
item.description: ICE agents killed the wrong man this morning in Biddeford, Maine, and left his body handcuffed in the street for five hours. Joan Sebastian Guerrero was 26, married, with a 3-year-old daughter. Agents staking out an address on his street rammed his car as he left for work and fired up to seven shots, four of them through his windshield. It took the DHS Secretary until nightfall to admit they had the wrong man. Six days earlier in Houston, agents killed Lorenzo Salgado Araujo, who was the wrong man too. Biddeford knew where to take its grief. By noon, several hundred people had marched down Main Street to Sen. Susan Collins' office, where some pushed into the entryway, pounding on her locked doors and chanting vote her out. Collins cast the deciding vote last month to award ICE $70 billion that runs through the end of Trump's term, and her office answered the protest by pointing to the accountability measures in the bill. The agents who killed Guerrero and Salgado Araujo were not wearing the cameras that accountability promised. I've emailed 8,213 stories over 529 straight nights, free and without ads. The algorithm can't show them all to you. Add your email address at govbrief.today and I will. Subscribe (It's free!): https://gbounacos.substack.com/ Today's full brief: https://go.govbrief.today/260713-full [1] https://go.govbrief.today/patricipation-trophy [2] https://go.govbrief.today/cuba-visit [3] https://go.govbrief.today/rubio-icc [4] https://go.govbrief.today/iran-war-july13 [5] https://go.govbrief.today/cyclo-greens [6] https://go.govbrief.today/utah-monuments [7] https://go.govbrief.today/doj-defense-leaks [8] https://go.govbrief.today/immigrants-credit [9] https://go.govbrief.today/trump-improper-purpose [10] https://go.govbrief.today/mpls-evidence [11] https://go.govbrief.today/ice-maine-shooting [12] https://go.govbrief.today/dhs-hack [13] https://go.govbrief.today/trump-sterling-health [14] https://go.govbrief.today/mcconnell-graham [15] https://go.govbrief.today/forest-rto [16] https://go.govbrief.today/biddeford-protests [17] https://go.govbrief.today/hostile-architecture [18] https://go.govbrief.today/states-paramount Subscribe (It's free!): https://gbounacos.substack.com/
item.dc:creator: GeorgeBounacos

item.dc:creator: svardfiska

item.title: Girl Power
item.description: A 31-year-old man who targeted more than half a dozen women by groping or chasing them through one of Portland’s most iconic parks faced a reckoning when the women — a “sisterhood” of strangers, according to one victim — surrounded the man, stopping him in his tracks. “We just started hitting, punching, scratching, pulling his hair, pulling on his clothes — everything,” said a 74-year-old victim, whom The Oregonian/OregonLive is not naming because of the nature of the crime. Seconds earlier she had kicked the man off her as he tried to crawl on top of her. https://www.oregonlive.com/crime/2026/07/half-dozen-women-hit-scratch-kick-portland-park-attacker-as-they-wait-31-minutes-for-police-to-respond.html
item.dc:creator: CrimeSceneShoe

item.dc:creator: MrShoemakers

item.title: First attempt at disguising myself with body paint - MCroft07
item.description: I've truly never done this before and it was really hard. Since I had to keep moving and realigning myself (which was really confusing) I chose to screenshot it without me in the frame and overlay that so I could realign myself easier each time I looked down to the mirror, that's why there is a ghosted effect. https://linktr.ee/mcroft07 if you want to see more because I don't post much here since I don't want to spam
item.dc:creator: MCroft07
